ddls:
CREATE TABLE "microcontrollers" (
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each microcontroller */,
  "mcu_model" TEXT /* Model name of the microcontroller */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the microcontroller */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the microcontroller is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the microcontroller was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the microcontroller (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Current firmware version of the microcontroller */,
  "last_check_in" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last check-in with the system */,
  "owner" TEXT /* Person responsible for the microcontroller */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments */,
  "maintenance_history_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the maintenance history ID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mcu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_microcontrollers_maintenance_history_id FOREIGN KEY ("maintenance_history_id") REFERENCES maintenance_logs ("maintenance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "adc_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the microcontroller ID */,
  "bit_number" INTEGER /* Bit number of the configuration setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration setting */,
  "value" TEXT /* Current value of the configuration setting (0: off, 1: on) */,
  "read_write" TEXT /* Read/Write status of the configuration setting */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the configuration setting */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the configuration setting */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value the configuration can take */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value the configuration can take */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the configuration value */,
  "description_extended" TEXT /* More detailed description of the configuration */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the configuration (e.g., Power, Temperature) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_adc_configurations_mcu_id FOREIGN KEY ("mcu_id") REFERENCES microcontrollers ("mcu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration setting ID */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "changed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the change */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Old value of the configuration setting before the change */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the configuration setting after the change */,
  "reason_for_change" TEXT /* Reason why the configuration was changed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the change was made */,
  "device_id" TEXT /* Device from which the change was made (e.g., mobile, web) */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the change was made (e.g., production, staging) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_logs_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES adc_configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user role ID */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of user creation */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user ID */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role ID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance activity */,
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the microcontroller ID */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., software update, hardware repair) */,
  "maintenance_details" TEXT /* Details of the maintenance activity */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the maintenance activity */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance activity (e.g., completed, in progress) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_mcu_id FOREIGN KEY ("mcu_id") REFERENCES microcontrollers ("mcu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomaly_detection" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each detected anomaly */,
  "mcu_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the microcontroller ID */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of anomaly (e.g., temperature spike, voltage drop) */,
  "anomaly_details" TEXT /* Details of the detected anomaly */,
  "detected_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the anomaly was detected */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the anomaly (e.g., resolved, open) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomaly_detection_mcu_id FOREIGN KEY ("mcu_id") REFERENCES microcontrollers ("mcu_id")
)
